openmeter/event/metadata.purpose: Defines shared event identity primitives: the canonical event name format (`io.openmeter.<subsystem>.<version>.<name>`), event metadata (ID, time, source, subject), and resource path helpers for constructing `//openmeter.io/...` URIs. All event payloads across the system reference these types for consistent naming and routing.

patterns.name: EventType triple (Subsystem/Version/Name)
patterns.description: Every event kind is declared as an `EventType{Subsystem, Version, Name}` value. The canonical string form is produced by `EventType.EventName()` — never build the string manually.

patterns.name: Resource paths via ComposeResourcePath
patterns.description: All `source` / `subject` fields in `EventMetadata` must be built with `ComposeResourcePath(namespace, entityType, id)` or `ComposeResourcePathRaw(...)`. Never construct `//openmeter.io/...` paths by hand.

patterns.name: Entity constant registry
patterns.description: All entity type strings (e.g. `EntityInvoice`, `EntityCustomer`) are declared as package-level constants. New entity types must be added here, not as inline strings in event producers.

key_file_guides.role: Canonical event naming: EventType struct + EventName()/VersionSubsystem() formatters, plus EventMetadata for ID/time/source/subject.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not bypass EventType.EventName() to build the name string; version is the second segment, not the third — the format is `<subsystem>.<version>.<name>`.

key_file_guides.role: Entity-type constants and ComposeResourcePath/ComposeResourcePathRaw helpers for all `//openmeter.io/...` URIs.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New domain entities need a matching `Entity*` constant added here; missing constants cause inconsistent resource paths across event sources.

anti_patterns: Building `io.openmeter.*` event name strings by hand instead of using EventType.EventName()
Constructing `//openmeter.io/...` paths with fmt.Sprintf instead of ComposeResourcePath
Adding a new entity type as an inline string literal in an event struct rather than adding an EntityXxx constant here

decisions.decision: EventName format encodes subsystem + version + name in a fixed dot-separated URI
decisions.rationale: Watermill consumers route and filter events by name prefix; a deterministic format prevents routing mismatches across worker binaries.
CloudEvents spec requires unambiguous source/subject URIs; the `//openmeter.io/` authority prefix scopes all paths to this system globally.

code_examples.scenario: Declaring a new event type and building its metadata

openmeter/event/models.purpose: Holds shared payload model types for events that cross subsystem boundaries (e.g. FeatureKeyAndID, NamespaceID). These are minimal value types with inline Validate() methods used inside event payloads, not domain entities.

patterns.name: Inline Validate() on every model
patterns.description: Each struct exported from this package implements `Validate() error` that checks required fields. Callers must invoke Validate() before using the value in an event payload.
patterns.example: if err := f.Validate(); err != nil { return fmt.Errorf("invalid feature ref: %w", err) }

patterns.name: Minimal shared types only
patterns.description: Only cross-subsystem value types belong here. Domain-specific structs belong in their own domain package, not in this shared models package.
patterns.example: FeatureKeyAndID{Key: "storage", ID: "feat_01"} — used in both entitlement and billing event payloads

key_file_guides.role: Declares FeatureKeyAndID and NamespaceID — small value types with Validate() used as embedded fields inside event payloads across subsystems.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not add domain-specific structs here; this package is imported by many subsystems so adding heavy types creates wide coupling.

anti_patterns: Adding domain-specific types (e.g. Invoice, Subscription) to this package — they belong in their own domain packages
Skipping Validate() calls on values received in event payloads
Importing openmeter domain packages from this package (creates circular dependencies)

decisions.decision: Separate package for cross-subsystem event model types
decisions.rationale: Prevents import cycles: both entitlement and billing packages can import event/models without pulling in each other's full domain.
